title: Search for the 1895 Samuel Dove Claar marriage and derivative evidence
repository: Federal census and Iowa marriage records accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1900 U.S. census, Cook Township, Decatur County, Kansas, enumeration district 8, page 2, household of Samuel Claar, NARA roll 477; Ancestry collection 7602, record 16623633. Iowa Department of Public Health, Iowa Marriage Records, 1880-1922, 1917 entry 25979; Ancestry collection 8823, record 1353470000.
transcription: The sought 1895 original marriage was not found in the searched Kansas collections. The 1900 census places Samuel and Maggie M. Claar in Decatur County, reports a five-year marriage, and says both were Iowa-born. A 1917 marriage entry for their daughter renders the mother's surname as Hayle, a derivative spelling that does not settle the Hoyle, Hogle, or Hayle conflict.

title: 1895 Decatur County marriage index entry for Samuel D. Claar and Maggie M. Hogle
repository: Decatur County, Kansas marriage records transcribed by KSGenWeb; originals held by Decatur County District Court
citation: Decatur County, Kansas Marriage Index, groom C-F and bride G-H views, Samuel D. Claar and Maggie M. Hogle, 5 October 1895, Oberlin; accessed 9 July 2026.
citation_gap: The two index views are one volunteer transcription, not independent records. The original county register or license has not been inspected, and the site warns that some transcribed names and dates may be questionable.
transcription: The groom and bride index views both render the couple as Samuel D. Claar and Maggie M. Hogle and give 5 October 1895 at Oberlin, Decatur County, Kansas. Because both views derive from one transcription, they establish a strong indexed place and date but do not independently settle the bride's surname. FamilySearch collection coverage omits Decatur County, explaining the earlier failed Kansas search and invalidating the resulting Iowa inference. The original county record remains the next pull for Hoyle, Hogle, or Hayle adjudication.

title: 1860, 1870, and 1880 census continuity of the David and Virginia Durham household
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1860 U.S. census, District 1, Muhlenberg County, Kentucky, dwelling 595, family 595, David Durham household; Ancestry collection 7667, record 39589780. 1870 U.S. census, Precinct 3, Muhlenberg County, Kentucky, Greenville post office, dwelling 32, David Durham household; Ancestry collection 7163, record 22194762. 1880 U.S. census, Nebo, Hopkins County, Kentucky, dwelling 227, D. M. Durham household; Ancestry collection 6742, record 10638953.
transcription: In 1860 David Durham was 26, born in North Carolina, and farming in Muhlenberg County dwelling and family 595. The extracted household sequence includes Virginia Durham, America E. Durham, and Nancy A. Durham; some age cells are shifted by the cockpit extraction, so the household names are preserved without silently reassigning every age. In 1870 David was 38, born in North Carolina, in Muhlenberg County dwelling 32 with Virginia A. and a child indexed as Marica L., age 12, followed by Nancy A., Mary T., Patrick M., and Milton. The name Marica L. is plausibly a handwriting or index reading of America E., whose expected age is about eleven or twelve, but the conflict is retained rather than normalized. In 1880 D. M. Durham was 51, born in Virginia, and head of the Nebo, Hopkins County dwelling 227 with wife Virginia A., 37, and children Mary I., 17; P. M., 14; D. M., 12; Louisa, 8; Nellie, 6; and Lilian M., 4. The wife's ages progress 17, 27, and 37 across the three extracts, and the younger children's names and ages progress coherently. David's reported birth year varies from about 1834 to 1832 to 1829, and his birthplace shifts from North Carolina to Virginia, but the repeated spouse, children, occupation, and adjacent-county geography strongly support one household head rather than two men. These censuses resolve the household identity through 1880; they do not decide whether David died in 1887 or 1897 and do not identify his parents.
